tool_5x_signature: enable_deterministic_mode(seed: int = 42, physics_dt: float = 1/60, solver_iterations: int = 4)
cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x, reproducible replay is configured via enable_deterministic_mode(seed=..., physics_dt=..., solver_iterations=...), which authors TGS solver + fixed timestep + CPU dynamics on /World/PhysicsScene. The 4.x omni.isaac.core.SimulationContext.set_deterministic method was removed in 5.0 and raises ImportError. PhysX floating-point ordering is hardware-dependent, so bit-identical replay is only guaranteed on the same GPU model + CUDA driver; the CI runner must be pinned. For rosbag-diff regression, follow the 'archive-with-version-pin' protocol: ship the Kit build hash, solver config, and seed alongside the USD stage as CI artifacts.
caveats: GPU dynamics (PhysX tensor view) is NOT fully deterministic even on identical hardware; CPU dynamics required.
Never claim 'fully deterministic' without the hardware-pin caveat.
Cross-machine determinism requires identical GPU model AND identical CUDA driver AND identical Kit build.
references: Isaac Sim 5.x release notes — solver TGS/PGS determinism section

cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x the ROS2 bridge moved from omni.isaac.ros2_bridge.* to isaacsim.ros2.nodes (from 5.0 onward; pre-5.0 5.x builds use isaacsim.ros2.bridge). The 4.x omni.isaac.ros2_bridge paths raise ModuleNotFoundError on 5.x. Use setup_ros2_bridge(profile=...) or configure_ros2_bridge(sensors=[...]) which auto-detect the correct namespace for the installed build. Note: rmw_init fails without AMENT_PREFIX_PATH in Kit's environment — Isaac Sim must be launched from a terminal that has sourced the ROS2 distro setup.bash.
caveats: OmniGraph node types use isaacsim.* namespace (NOT omni.isaac.*); use the composite tools that handle the version split.
AMENT_PREFIX_PATH must be set in Kit's process environment (source /opt/ros/<distro>/setup.bash before launching).
references: Isaac Sim ROS2 Bridge Migration Guide — v4 → v5

cite: In Isaac Sim 5.x, URDF import uses isaacsim.asset.importer.urdf.import_urdf(asset_path, cfg) or the registered import_robot tool. The 4.x omni.isaac.urdf and later omni.importer.urdf module paths were consolidated under isaacsim.asset.importer. URDFParseAndImportFile remains available as an omni.kit.commands entry but silently returns (result=False, prim_path=None) on missing file or parse error — always pair it with HasAuthoredReferences / stage.GetPrimAtPath(dest).IsValid() post-checks (import_robot tool already does this).
caveats: URDFParseAndImportFile success signal is a tuple; empty prim_path means failure even with result=True in some Kit versions.
cfg.convex_decomposition=False (convex hull) is the safe default for collision meshes.

physx_rule_5x: ArticulationRootAPI must be authored on the ROBOT ROOT PRIM (not a parent Xform group)
cite: Isaac Sim 5.x's PhysX tensor API (used by ArticulationView for batched RL) pattern-matches on the prim path where PhysicsArticulationRootAPI is applied. Moving the API to a parent Xform (e.g. /World/envs instead of /World/envs/env_0/Franka) breaks tensor-view registration silently — trajectories read as zeros. The 4.x omni.isaac.core.articulations.Articulation class was replaced by isaacsim.core.prims.SingleArticulation in 5.x. For cloning, use isaacsim.core.cloner.GridCloner which preserves the articulation-root marker on each clone's robot root.
caveats: Never move ArticulationRootAPI off the robot root — ArticulationView.initialize fails silently.
GridCloner is for layout batching, NOT the same as Replicator's DR cloner (different purposes).
